# Extracao: sub list 3

- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0 MB

SUBJECT: Double "oops" !!

<firstname>

<sigh>

OK, now I *know* that I need to start getting some
more sleep...

A couple of hours ago I sent you a message about
how I had made a mistake with posting the "launch"
date for my new copywriting course.

The only problem was that I made ANOTHER mistake
in that email.

Here is the real date, with no mistakes this time:

>> Wednesday OCTOBER 18th at noon eastern time <<


I am sorry to bother you again, and I am going to
step away from the keyboard now so I can be sure I
don't make any more mistakes today.

If you want to go to the blog and make fun of me
with a comment, here is the link:

http://blog.creatingcopythatsells.info


best regards,
Bill Glazer


P.S. I am sure there are some people that think
that these "oops" messages are just a marketing
trick. They aren't... although there is a lesson
to be learned here about making lemonade out of
lemons. But believe me, I sure don't like sending
two "oops" messages in one day! 

The point is, we are all human. And that's good,
because the people reading your copy want to
connect to humans... they don't want to buy from
faceless companies. So when you admit a mistake -
especially one that is just a simple typo - you
show that you are very human. And that actaully
helps to create a bit of a human connection with
your reader.

P.P.S. I am sure there are also a few other people
that think "how unprofessional" - they think that
anyone in my position should never make mistakes.
Well, I'm sorry... as I mentioned above, I am
human. I don't make any claim on omnipotence. I
make typos all the time.

However, I also write million dollar sales letters
pretty darn frequently. :-)

And my copywriting students have been knocking
them dead as well.

P.P.P.S. Once and for all, it is WEDNESDAY,
OCTOBER 18th. At 12:00 noon.   :-)

http://blog.creatingcopythatsells.info
